purpose: Provides thermal insulation and structural support in high-temperature furnace applications, preventing heat loss and maintaining thermal efficiency

inputs.name: heat_transfer
inputs.description: Heat flux through the insulation material from the furnace interior

states.name: linear_shrinkage
states.description: Cumulative linear shrinkage from thermal exposure at maximum temperature

states.name: thermal_conductivity
states.description: Thermal conductivity that increases with temperature and degradation

states.name: insulation_integrity
states.description: Overall insulation effectiveness accounting for shrinkage and gaps

events.name: reduced_temperature_capability_hydrogen
events.description: Maximum continuous duty temperature reduced in hydrogen atmosphere

events.name: radiant_heat_loss_through_gaps
events.description: Heat loss through gaps between modules due to tolerances and shrinkage

parameters.name: shrinkage_rate
parameters.description: Rate of linear shrinkage accumulation per unit heat flux

parameters.name: max_shrinkage
parameters.description: Maximum allowable linear shrinkage before complete failure

parameters.name: conductivity_tracking_coeff
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for thermal conductivity tracking

parameters.name: base_conductivity
parameters.description: Base thermal conductivity at low temperature

parameters.name: conductivity_heat_factor
parameters.description: Factor relating heat flux to conductivity increase

parameters.name: shrinkage_degradation_time
parameters.description: Time constant for insulation integrity degradation

parameters.name: integrity_shrinkage_factor
parameters.description: Factor relating shrinkage to integrity loss

parameters.name: temp_capability_threshold
parameters.description: Insulation integrity threshold for temperature capability warning

parameters.name: heat_loss_shrinkage_threshold
parameters.description: Linear shrinkage threshold for heat loss failure

parameters.name: heat_loss_integrity_threshold
parameters.description: Insulation integrity threshold for heat loss failure

state_transition_logic: linear_shrinkage increases by shrinkage_rate * heat_transfer * dt, clamped to [0.0, max_shrinkage]
thermal_conductivity approaches (base_conductivity + conductivity_heat_factor * heat_transfer) using first-order lag with coefficient conductivity_tracking_coeff, clamped to [0.05, 0.15]
insulation_integrity decreases by (1/shrinkage_degradation_time) * heat_transfer * dt + integrity_shrinkage_factor * linear_shrinkage * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
event_logic: radiant_heat_loss_through_gaps occurs when linear_shrinkage > heat_loss_shrinkage_threshold OR insulation_integrity < heat_loss_integrity_threshold
assumptions: Linear shrinkage is primarily driven by thermal cycling and high heat flux exposure
Thermal conductivity increases with temperature and can be approximated by heat flux
Insulation integrity degrades due to both thermal exposure and physical shrinkage
Heat loss through gaps becomes significant after 4.5% linear shrinkage
Temperature capability is reduced when integrity falls below 80%
